title: Zygophlebia villosissima
taxonomy: Zygophlebia villosissima is a species of fern in the family Lomariopsidaceae. It belongs to the genus Zygophlebia. The classification of Zygophlebia villosissima is as follows: Kingdom: Plantae, Division: Pteridophyta, Class: Polypodiopsida, Order: Polypodiales, Family: Lomariopsidaceae, Genus: Zygophlebia, and Species: Z. villosissima. Further classification within the family Lomariopsidaceae places it in the tribe Lomariopsidinae. This fern is native to tropical regions of Central and South America.

intro: Zygophlebia villosissima, also known as the Villose Fork-Leaved Fern, is a species of fern in the family Polypodiaceae. This plant has been used in traditional medicine, particularly in the Amazon region, for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which may help in treating various ailments such as wounds and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Z. villosissima include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Z. villosissima is often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, as well as being incorporated into topical creams and ointments to aid in wound healing and skin issues.
